title: Dynamic array (table doubling): push is amortized O(1)
statement_informal: A dynamic array doubles its capacity when full. A push costs 1; on overflow it copies all c elements before doubling. By the potential method (Phi = 2*size - capacity), n pushes from a fresh array cost at most 3n total: da_cost n 0 1 <= 3n. The expensive O(n) copy is amortized away by the cheap pushes that precede it.

cost_model.notes: da_cost n s c = total cost of n pushes at size s, capacity c. Push = 1; resize (s=c) adds c copies and doubles capacity. Proved by the potential method with invariant 1<=c, c/2 <= s <= c and amortized cost 3 per push.

references: CLRS 3rd ed. §17.4 (dynamic tables) — table doubling gives amortized O(1) insertion by the accounting / potential method.
